evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/rime.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site positions Rime as voice models for real-time conversations, listing 600+ voices, 50+ languages, and sub-100ms latency.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The official navigation exposes pricing, docs, demo booking, login, and free signup, making the product workflow inspectable beyond a waitlist.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: The funding source says Rime is moving toward speech-to-speech models to improve latency, turn-taking, and background-noise handling for enterprise calls.

risks: Research risk candidate: The falsifiable risk is whether Rime's speech-to-speech models can maintain natural turn-taking and low latency when deployed across noisy enterprise call-center traffic.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/oak.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site describes one platform that sees and governs human, machine, and AI-agent identities from a single live graph.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: Oak exposes product pages for an identity intelligence layer, AI-agent governance, universal connectors, white-glove migration, and a demo flow.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: The funding source says Oak maps access to application usage and can remove no-longer-needed permissions in real time.

risks: Research risk candidate: The falsifiable risk is whether Oak can build connector coverage and remediation trust quickly enough for security teams to let it change live permissions.
Research risk candidate: TechCrunch names Accel, CRV, and Greylock as co-leads; Accel is used as the lead investor field to keep the single-lead schema resolvable.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/emergent.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official app-builder page says users can turn natural-language prompts into production-ready websites, apps, dashboards, and mobile builds.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The official pricing page exposes Free, Standard, Pro, and Enterprise plans with credits, private project hosting, GitHub integration, fork tasks, larger context, and system-prompt editing.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: The funding source says Emergent has more than 200,000 paying customers and examples spanning trucking, factories, construction, and property-management businesses.

risks: Research risk candidate: The falsifiable risk is whether apps generated for non-technical SMBs remain debuggable, secure, and maintainable after real customer data and workflow exceptions arrive.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/applied-computing.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site says Orbital monitors thousands of sensors, forecasts outcomes, finds optimal set-points, and informs early equipment warnings in real time.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The official site describes Orbital connecting to DCS, historians, and LIMS while constraining learned plant behavior with physics.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: The funding source says Orbital combines time-series data, physics-based models, and language models to flag anomalies, investigate causes, and model proposed fixes.

risks: Research risk candidate: The falsifiable risk is whether Orbital can scale from expert-supported deployments to repeatable plant onboarding across different assets, process histories, and data quality levels.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/linker-finance.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site says Linker supports digital account opening in under 3 minutes with KYC, fraud checks, core-account creation, and account funding.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The official site lists business onboarding, retail banking, commercial banking, Positive Pay, Treasury Management, and RDC capabilities.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: The funding source says Linker is live with more than 10 customers across multiple cores, including Fiserv Premier, CSI, FIS, and soon DCI.

risks: Research risk candidate: The funding announcement does not name a formal lead; Chingona Ventures is used as the representative lead because it is the first named returning investor with a sourced quote.
Research risk candidate: A second open question is whether the emerging AI and stablecoin-enabled payment capabilities distract from the clearer community-bank onboarding wedge.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/instalily.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site describes Lily as an AI forward-deployed engineer that learns a business, builds needed software, deploys it, and stays current.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The official site lists operational systems and data sources including SAP, NetSuite, Epicor, Salesforce, HubSpot, Snowflake, inboxes, and PDFs.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: The official research post says Lily is live in construction, industrial distribution, logistics, field service, and healthcare operations.

risks: Research risk candidate: The falsifiable risk is whether each customer deployment can remain productized enough to avoid a labor-heavy forward-deployed engineering model.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/hadrius.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site groups marketing review, communications archiving, trade surveillance, employee oversight, and firm oversight in one compliance platform.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The communications page says Hadrius supports email, chat, and social supervision with zero-retention AI, WORM archiving, and immutable timelines.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: The funding source says Hadrius reduces false positives by 95%, manual compliance work by 70%, and saves 20+ hours per week.

risks: Research risk candidate: The falsifiable risk is whether Hadrius can keep its review rules, integrations, and evidence packs current across each regulator and firm policy set.
